phrases.intent_en: Ask about the antique market schedule
phrases.text_local: Quand a lieu le marché aux antiquités ?
phrases.pronunciation: kohn ah lee-OO luh mar-SHAY oh zon-tee-kee-TAY
phrases.cultural_note: The Sablon antique market is held every Saturday and Sunday. Over 30 antique dealers sell jewelry, art, furniture, and collectibles.

responses.phonetic: sahm-DEE ay dee-MAHNSH, duh nuhf uhr ah kah-torz uhr
responses.french: Samedi et dimanche, de neuf heures à quatorze heures.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the best chocolate shops are
phrases.cultural_note: The Sablon area is famous for its chocolate shops, including Wittamer and Pierre Marcolini.

responses.english: Wittamer and Pierre Marcolini are on the square. Don't miss them.
responses.phonetic: vee-TAH-mair ay pyehr mar-koh-LEE-nee son soor lah PLAHS. ah nuh pah mahn-KAY
responses.french: Wittamer et Pierre Marcolini sont sur la place. À ne pas manquer.

phrases.text_local: Y a-t-il de bons restaurants gastronomiques ?
phrases.cultural_note: The Sablon is known for upscale dining. 'Comme chez Soi' (two Michelin stars) and 'La Vie en Rose' are local highlights.

responses.english: Yes, there are several on the square and nearby.
responses.phonetic: wee, eel ee ahn ah plu-ZYUHR soor lah PLAHS ay oh zah-lahn-TOOR
responses.french: Oui, il y en a plusieurs sur la place et aux alentours.

phrases.text_local: Y a-t-il une église à proximité ?
phrases.cultural_note: Notre-Dame du Sablon (15th century) is a beautiful Gothic church nearby, worth a quick visit for its interior stained glass.

responses.english: Yes, Notre-Dame du Sablon, right next door. Free entry.
responses.phonetic: wee, notr-DAHM doo sah-BLON, zhoost ah koh-TAY. ahn-TRAY grah-TWEET
responses.french: Oui, Notre-Dame du Sablon, juste à côté. Entrée gratuite.
